messages.content:
You are an AI assistant that completes tasks by reasoning and using tools.

## Think Before Acting

Before making tool calls, briefly explain your reasoning in 1-3 sentences:
- What you learned from the previous step
- What you're doing next and why

Don't over-explain. Be concise but show your thinking.

## Tools

**Always Available (Meta-Tools):**
- `todo_write` - Task planning: create/update todos. Takes `todos` array [{id, content, status}] and `merge` boolean.
- `toolbelt_list_tools` / `toolbelt_inspect_tool` / `toolbelt_add_tool` / `toolbelt_remove_tool` - Tool management
- `final_answer` - Submit your answer (status: completed/blocked/failed)

**Domain Tools:** Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to discover, then `toolbelt_add_tool` to add them.

## Workflow

1. Plan: Use `todo_write` to create todos for complex tasks
2. Discover: Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to find relevant tools
3. Execute: Work through todos, use `todo_write` with `merge=true` to update status
4. Complete: Call `final_answer` (all todos must be completed/cancelled first)

## Rules

- Update todo status with `todo_write`: set `in_progress` when starting, `completed` when done
- Show your work for calculations
- `final_answer` is rejected if todos are incomplete

## Reference cheatsheet — formulas, conventions, environment facts, pitfalls

The block below is a **reference cheatsheet** — like a formula sheet and
a page of environment notes clipped to your desk. It collects reusable
formulas, definitions, conventions, documented behaviours of the tools
and execution surface, output-delivery conventions, and pitfall flags
that earlier agents working structurally similar cases found worth
writing down.

**This is reference material, not instructions.** Treat it the way you'd
treat a formula sheet and a notes page during an exam:

  • You do not "follow" a cheatsheet. You consult it when you need a
    specific fact, and otherwise ignore it.
  • Entries are passive reminders, not rules. None of them command you
    to do anything, and none of them describe the steps for this case.
  • Your own reading of the current task is authoritative. If an entry
    suggests a formula, convention, tool-argument form, or delivery
    channel that conflicts with what this task asks for, follow the
    task.
  • Many entries will be irrelevant to this case. That is expected for a
    cheatsheet. Skip them without comment.
  • Do not change a value, formula, methodology, tool-call shape,
    precision, or delivery form you have arrived at independently just
    because an entry mentions something different. Entries describe what
    was useful on *other* cases.

The cheatsheet exists only to spare you from re-deriving a useful
formula, to remind you of a tool or environment behaviour you might
otherwise re-discover the slow way (for example by hitting an avoidable
error first), or to flag a known trap. Work the current case as you
normally would. Do not cite, quote, or mention the cheatsheet in your
answer or your reasoning.

## Reusable Code and Tool-Call Snippets
<memory_item>
<description>
To extract specific text from a PDF (such as statute sections or legal provisions), first call pdf_server_pdf with action="search" using the key term or citation as query (case-insensitive), then follow with action="read_pages" on the returned page number(s). Use max_results=5 and context_chars=100 for search.
</description>
<example>
pdf_server_pdf({"request":{"action":"search","file_path":"/Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE>/<pdfname>.pdf","query":"194A.713","case_sensitive":false,"max_results":5,"context_chars":100}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To read multi-page .docx agreements, call docs_server_docs with action="read_content" and page_index (starting at 0) to retrieve paragraphs sequentially; repeat for additional pages as needed. Paths must start with / and end with .docx.
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"read_content","file_path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts/<agreement>.docx","page_index":0}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To create a new .docx document (e.g., a legal memorandum or analysis), call docs_server_docs with action="create", specifying directory (folder path) and file_name (ending with .docx) separately, along with content as an array of valid content blocks (each object must have a "type" field such as "heading" or "paragraph" with required fields populated; empty objects or incorrect parameter names like file_path will fail validation).
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"create","directory":"/Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/Tom Buchanan","file_name":"Memo.docx","content":[{"type":"heading","text":"Title","level":1},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Body text here."}]}})
</example>
</memory_item>

## Solution Strategies for Recurring Task Shapes
<memory_item>
<description>
For compliance reviews of assisted living residency agreements against state statutes, first locate and extract the exact statutory requirement(s) from the state PDF, then read the target agreement document(s) page-by-page, and compare each enumerated statutory element against the agreement language for presence or omission. When multiple similar agreement files exist, select the most general or state-matched version rather than a state-specific variant.
</description>
<example>
Locate statute PDF → search/read target section → read agreement → map each statutory item (e.g., notice periods, fee provisions) to agreement text.
</example>
</memory_item>

## Formulas, Definitions, and Conventions
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(7) and analogous state provisions require a minimum 30-day notice for fee structure changes in assisted living lease agreements; absence of any such notice language constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (7): "Minimum thirty (30) day notice provision for a change in the community's fee structure."
</example>
</memory_item>

## Environment and Sandbox Facts
<memory_item>
<description>
State statutes for assisted living compliance are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE ABBREV>/ as PDFs (e.g., 194A_700-7205.pdf for Kentucky). Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Reorganization/State Statutes/ to discover available states.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/KY/ contains 194A_700-7205.pdf and Regs 902 KAR 20 480.pdf
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Magnolia-specific agreements reside in /Magnolia Documents/Contracts/; files include General Resident Agreement Template.docx and state-variant versions (e.g., Gardens Residency Agreement Martin Miller v1.docx for California).
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts"})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Litigation-related documents, including complaints, strategies, and case files for wrongful death, breach of contract, and assisted living matters, are stored under /Litigation Strategy/ with subfolders such as Assisted Living Litigation in General/ (containing PDFs), Breach of Contract Documents/ (containing complaints and related files), and Wrongful Death Documents/. Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Litigation Strategy/ to discover available files.
</description>
<example>
/Litigation Strategy/ contains Assisted Living Litigation in General/, Breach of Contract Documents/, and Wrongful Death Documents/
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Breach of contract complaints and related filings are stored under /Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents/ with possible subfolders such as Complaints/. Use filesystem_server_list_files on that path to locate original, first amended, and second amended versions.
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents"})
</example>
</memory_item>

## Pitfalls, Edge Cases, and Verification Checks
<memory_item>
<description>
When multiple residency agreement files exist, verify the selected file matches the task scope (general template vs. state-specific or resident-specific version) before comparison; California RCFE forms may contain 60-day notice language that satisfies a 30-day minimum but is irrelevant to other states.
</description>
<example>
Confirm agreement file name and content scope before mapping to statute (e.g., avoid using CA-specific form for KY compliance check).
</example>
</memory_item>

---
Susan Whitaker has filed three successive Complaints against Magnolia Gardens. The first is the original Complaint, the second Is the First Amended Complaint, and the third is the Second Amended Complaint. Each Complaint was served on the defendant ten days after they were filed. Magnolia Gardens wants to file a Motion to Dismiss the breach of contract allegations in the Complaint. 

Review the complaints and decide if, under Texas Rule of Civil Procedure 91a.3, what is the last day the motion to dismiss can be timely filed? When was the original complaint served?
Give me your answer right here.

{"action":"read_pages","error":null,"help":null,"create":null,"read_pages":{"raw_output":"[pdf: pages=351, read=1]

[page 70]
Page 52 
 
reasonably drawn from them, do not entitle the claimant to the relief sought.  A cause of 
action has no basis in fact if no reasonable person could believe the facts pleaded. 
 
91a.2 Contents of Motion.  A motion to dismiss must state that it is made pursuant to this rule, 
must identify each cause of action to which it is addressed, and must state specifically the 
reasons the cause of action has no basis in law, no basis in fact, or both. 
 
91a.3 Time for Motion and Ruling.  A motion to dismiss must be: 
 
(a) 
filed within 60 days after the first pleading containing the challenged cause of 
action  is served on the movant; 
 
(b) 
filed at least 21 days before the motion is heard; and 
 
(c) 
granted or denied within 45 days after the motion is filed. 
 
91a.4 Time for Response.  Any response to the motion must be filed no later than 7 days before 
the date of the hearing. 
 
91a.5 Effect of Nonsuit or Amendment; Withdrawal of Motion. 
 
(a) 
The court may not rule on a motion to dismiss if, at least 3 days before the date of 
the hearing, the respondent files a nonsuit of the challenged cause of action, or the 
movant files a withdrawal of the motion. 
 
(b) 
If the respondent amends the challenged cause of action at least 3 days before the 
date of the hearing, the movant may, before the date of the hearing, file a withdrawal 
of  
the motion or an amended motion directed to the amended cause of action. 
 
(c) 
Except by agreement of the parties, the court must rule on a motion unless it has 
been withdrawn or the cause of action has been nonsuited in accordance with (a) or 
(b).  In ruling on the motion, the court must not consider a nonsuit or amendment 
not filed as permitted by paragraphs (a) or (b). 
 
(d) 
An amended motion filed in accordance with (b) restarts the time periods in this 
rule. 
 
91a.6 Hearing; No Evidence Considered.  Each party is entitled to at least 14 days’ notice of 
the hearing on the motion to dismiss.  The court may, but is not required to, conduct an 
oral hearing on the motion.  Except as required by 91a.7, the court may not consider 
evidence in ruling on the motion and must decide the motion based solely on the pleading 
of the cause of action, together with any pleading exhibits permitted by Rule 59. 
 
91a.7 Award of Costs and Attorney Fees. Except in an action by or against a governmental 
entity or a public official acting in his or her official capacity or under color of law, the 
"},"search":null}

{"action":"read_pages","error":null,"help":null,"create":null,"read_pages":{"raw_output":"[pdf: pages=351, read=1]

[page 20]
Page 2 
 
Notes and Comments 
Comment to 1990 change: To make Texas Rules of Civil Procedure timetables mandatory 
and to preclude use of unpublished local rules or other “standing” orders to local practices to 
determine issues of substantive merit.  
Comment to 2023 change: Rule 3a is amended to remove the requirement that the Supreme 
Court of Texas approve local rules and to expressly address local forms and standing orders. The 
amended rule provides that local rules, forms, and standing orders must not conflict with other 
laws or rules and that they are not effective unless published on the Office of Court 
Administration’s website. Section 74.093(b) of the Texas Government Code imposes additional 
requirements for local rules. 
 
RULE 4.  COMPUTATION OF TIME 
 
In computing any period of time prescribed or allowed by these rules, by order of court, or by any 
applicable statute, the day of the act, event, or default after which the designated period of time 
begins to run is not to be included. The last day of the period so computed is to be included, unless 
it is a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, in which event the period runs until the end of the next 
day which is not a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday. Saturdays, Sundays, and legal holidays shall 
not be counted for any purpose in any time period of five days or less in these rules, except that 
Saturdays, Sundays, and legal holidays shall be counted for purpose of the three-day periods in 
Rules 21 and 21a, extending other periods by three days when service is made by mail. 
 
Notes and Comments 
 
Comment to 1990 change: Amended to omit counting Saturdays, Sundays and legal 
holidays in all periods of less than five days with certain exceptions. 
 
RULE 5.  ENLARGEMENT OF TIME 
 
When by these rules or by a notice given thereunder or by order of court an act is required or 
allowed to be done at or within a specified time, the court for cause shown may, at any time in its 
discretion (a) with or without motion or notice, order the period enlarged if application therefor is 
made before the expiration of the period originally prescribed or as extended by a previous order; 
or (b) upon motion permit the act to be done after the expiration of the specified period where good 
cause is shown for the failure to act. The court may not enlarge the period for taking any action 
under the rules relating to new trials except as stated in these rules. If any document is sent to the 
proper clerk by first-class United States mail in an envelope or wrapper properly addressed and 
stamped and is deposited in the mail on or before the last day for filing same, the same, if received 
by the clerk not more than ten days tardily, shall be filed by the clerk and be deemed filed in time. 
A legible postmark affixed by the United States Postal Service shall be prima facie evidence of the 
date of mailing. 
"},"search":null}

messages.reasoning_content: Key: "the day of the act, event, or default after which the designated period of time begins to run is not to be included."

[body.p.4] (Heading 2): PLAINTIFF’S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

[body.p.5] (normal): 
Filed: February 14, 2026


[body.p.6] (Heading 2): I. INTRODUCTION

[body.p.7] (normal): Plaintiff files this Second Amended Complaint to refine and clarify factual allegations regarding Defendant’s material breaches of the 2023 Residential Care Agreement governing the assisted living placement of Thomas Whitaker at The Grove–Austin. Plaintiff continues to bring a single cause of action for breach of contract.

[body.p.8] (Heading 2): II. PARTIES, JURISDICTION, AND VENUE

[body.p.9] (normal): 1. Plaintiff Susan Whitaker is an individual residing in Travis County, Texas. Plaintiff entered into the 2023 Residential Care Agreement on behalf of her father, Thomas Whitaker.

[body.p.10] (normal): 2. Defendant Magnolia Gardens, Inc. is a Delaware corporation headquartered at 500 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 3200, Chicago, Illinois 60611. Defendant operates The Grove–Austin at 2900 Riverside Drive, Austin, Texas 78704.

[body.p.11] (normal): 3. Venue and jurisdiction are proper in this Court because the contract was executed, performed, and breached in Travis County, Texas.

[body.p.12] (Heading 2): III. CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS

[body.p.13] (normal): Under the 2023 Residential Care Agreement, Defendant undertook several material obligations, including but not limited to the following:

[body.p.14] (normal): • Maintaining a resident-to-staff ratio not to exceed eight-to-one (8:1).

[body.p.15] (normal): • Conducting comprehensive Care Plan assessments upon admission and every three (3) months thereafter.

[body.p.16] (normal): • Providing three nutritionally balanced meals each day.

[body.p.17] (normal): • Offering a monthly calendar of regular social, recreational, and wellness activities.

[body.p.18] (normal): • Delivering all services outlined in the individualized Care Plan.

[body.p.19] (normal): • Providing at least sixty (60) days’ written notice prior to any material change affecting staffing, services, activities, or fees.

[body.p.20] (Heading 2): IV. FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS

[body.p.21] (normal): 4. In or around February 2025, Defendant implemented a series of operational changes at The Grove–Austin affecting staffing, meals, reassessments, activity offerings, and performance of Care Plan items.

[body.p.22] (normal): 5. Following these changes, Plaintiff observed and received reports of delays in essential care services due to insufficient staffing, particularly during early morning and evening hours.

[body.p.23] (normal): 6. Around mid-February 2025, breakfast service was reduced from a prepared hot meal to a limited continental offering without prior written notice to residents or their families.

[body.p.24] (normal): 7. Beginning in March 2025, the number and frequency of scheduled activities decreased substantially, contrary to the Agreement’s assurances regarding social and recreational programming.

[body.p.25] (normal): 8. Plaintiff later discovered that Thomas Whitaker’s required quarterly reassessment for 2025 had not taken place within the contractually mandated timeframe.

[body.p.26] (normal): 9. During this period, core Care Plan services—such as mobility assistance and timely medication reminders—were not consistently provided.

[body.p.27] (normal): 10. Despite these material changes, Defendant did not provide the sixty (60) days’ written notice required under the Agreement.

[body.p.28] (normal): 11. Plaintiff continued to pay all required fees, except for a temporary rate adjustment implemented by Defendant in May 2025.

[body.p.29] (normal): 12. Defendant’s actions deprived Plaintiff and her father of the benefit of their bargain under the Agreement.

[body.p.30] (Heading 2): V. CAUSE OF ACTION — BREACH OF CONTRACT

[body.p.31] (normal): 13. Plaintiff incorporates paragraphs 1–12 by reference as though fully set forth herein.

[body.p.32] (normal): 14. Defendant breached the Agreement by doing one or more of the following:

[body.p.33] (normal): a) Failing to maintain the required 8:1 staffing ratio;

[body.p.34] (normal): b) Failing to conduct quarterly reassessments;

[body.p.35] (normal): c) Failing to provide three nutritionally balanced meals of comparable quality;

[body.p.36] (normal): d) Failing to maintain a consistent schedule of social and recreational activities;

[body.p.37] (normal): e) Failing to deliver services outlined in the individualized Care Plan;

[body.p.38] (normal): f) Failing to provide sixty (60) days’ written notice before implementing material changes;

[body.p.39] (normal): g) Failing to maintain service levels reasonably expected under the Agreement.

[body.p.40] (normal): 15. Defendant’s breaches caused Plaintiff to suffer damages, including payment for services not rendered, diminished value of contractual services, and loss of the benefit of the bargain.

[body.p.41] (Heading 2): VI. PRAYER FOR RELIEF

[body.p.42] (normal): Plaintiff respectfully requests that Defendant be cited to appear and answer, and that upon final trial, Plaintiff recover actual damages, attorneys’ fees under Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 38.001, court costs, and all other relief to which she is justly entitled.

[body.p.43] (normal): 
Respectfully submitted,
Plaintiff’s Counsel
"},"read_image":null,"add_text":null,"edit_text":null,"delete_text":null,"add_image":null,"modify_image":null,"format":null,"header_footer":null,"page_margins":null,"page_orientation":null,"comments":null}
